mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing three months raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a specific threshold to be crossed.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of rising FEDFUNDS show statistically indistinguishable returns from periods of falling FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days). Below this threshold the signal is inactive.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered-period return is not significantly lower than the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects synchronized strength in consumer and residential investment demand, a combination that individually weaker readings do not capture; this joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because it indicates broad-based demand momentum rather than a single noisy sector.
failure_condition: The joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the maximum of the two individual single-series Rank ICs by more than 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression for manufacturers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). In early-cycle regimes the same deceleration is not predictive because it reflects re-acceleration from a low base rather than exhaustion.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth-return relationship fails to hold (correlation flips sign or Rank IC < 0.02) when restricted to independently-identified late-cycle expansion windows over the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a real-time proxy for labor income growth and near-term consumption capacity; positive payroll surprises produce a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets price in stronger corporate revenue expectations. The effect is continuous and always active.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly different from returns following negative surprises (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below a threshold decline of more than 10% year-over-year, it signals a contraction in residential investment that historically leads broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Below this threshold magnitude the signal has no predictive activation.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline events are not followed by below-median 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of historical occurrences, or Rank IC in triggered windows falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising FEDFUNDS with elevated CPIAUCSL inflation readings signals a policy tightening cycle aimed at controlling inflation that is not yet reflected in nominal rates alone; this joint condition compresses forward equity valuations more than either series in isolation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL signal's Rank IC does not exceed the single-series FEDFUNDS Rank IC by more than 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no incremental information.

mechanism_narrative: In a consumer-led expansion regime characterized by steady retail sales growth, positive month-over-month RSAFS readings produce a short-term positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets extrapolate near-term consumer spending strength into earnings expectations. In regimes of weak consumer demand (e.g., contracting retail sales trend) this relationship inverts or disappears.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than following negative RSAFS growth (p > 0.05) when restricted to independently identified consumer-led expansion periods.
